data.title: Europe Strengthens Security Ahead of French Presidential Election
data.description: Europe is intensifying efforts to protect its key institutions as France approaches its presidential election. Bloomberg posted on X that recent surveys indicate a significant lead for the far-right National Rally party. This development has prompted European nations to bolster security measures to ensure the stability and integrity of their political and economic systems. The heightened precautions reflect concerns over potential disruptions that could arise from the election outcome. Authorities are focused on maintaining order and safeguarding democratic processes during this critical period.

data.title: Bitcoin's Shutdown Price: Understanding Its Role in Mining Operations
data.description: The concept of Bitcoin's 'shutdown price' is often discussed in the context of mining operations. According to NS3.AI, this price level is perceived as the threshold below which miners may cease their activities. The article examines whether this shutdown price truly represents the bottom line for miners or merely serves as a market filter amid volatile conditions. It seeks to address common misconceptions surrounding this term in relation to Bitcoin mining and market dynamics.

data.title: Uniswap Labs Launches Beta Developer Platform for Swift Integration
data.description: Uniswap Labs has introduced the beta version of its Developer Platform, designed to streamline the integration of Uniswap's swapping and liquidity functions through API keys. According to NS3.AI, this platform allows developers to incorporate these functionalities into their applications swiftly, with integration possible within minutes. Furthermore, the platform includes a new Skill feature that supports proxy-based workflows, offering one-click API access via command line tools.

data.title: Ethical Concerns Impact Progress of CLARITY Act in Crypto Legislation
data.description: Ethical concerns surrounding the Trump family's involvement in cryptocurrency are affecting the advancement of the CLARITY Act, a crucial piece of legislation aimed at structuring the crypto market. According to NS3.AI, Caitlin Long has pointed out that opposition, particularly from Senator Elizabeth Warren, poses a threat to the bipartisan support essential for the bill's passage. Despite these challenges, Senators Cynthia Lummis and Kirsten Gillibrand are continuing their bipartisan efforts to advance crypto regulatory reform.

data.title: Bithumb will list Aztek (AZTEC) Korean Won trading pair
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bithumb will launch a trading pair for Aztek (AZTEC) Korean Won.

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: Bitcoin ETFs have seen net inflows of $53 billion over two years, with Wall Street influence continuing to be positive.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, pointed out in an article on the X platform that the cumulative net inflow of Bitcoin ETFs (the most important data) peaked at $63 billion last October and is currently at $53 billion, meaning a net increase of $53 billion in just two years. He stated that his team (more optimistic than most of their peers) originally predicted inflows of only $5-15 billion in the first year. This data is of great reference value for understanding the $8 billion ETF outflow after Bitcoin's 45% drop and the relationship between Bitcoin and Wall Street. Currently, the overall influence of Wall Street continues to be positive.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $130 million yesterday, with BlackRock's ETHA experiencing the largest net outflow at $96.7977 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $130 million yesterday (February 19th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net outflow of $96.7977 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $11.873 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF ETH, with a single-day net outflow of $18.4365 million. ETH's historical total net inflow has reached $1.702 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $11.004 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.67%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.525 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $166 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $166 million yesterday (February 19th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (IBIT) experienced the largest single-day net outflow, at $164 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $61.238 billion. The Valkyrie ETF (BRRR) followed with a net outflow of $1.7013 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $314 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $84.37 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.28%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $53.925 billion.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Former South Korean President Yoon Seok-yeol Responds to Martial Law Decision and Life Sentence
data.description: Former South Korean President Yoon Seok-yeol has expressed his stance on the declaration of martial law, stating it was a 'salvation decision' made for the nation and its citizens. According to Jin10, Yoon conveyed his sentiments through his legal team, acknowledging the difficulties his actions may have caused due to his 'own shortcomings.' Additionally, Yoon has publicly rejected the life sentence handed down in his first trial, indicating his inability to accept the verdict.

data.title: Seoul Apartment Prices Continue to Rise Amid Central Bank Rate Review
data.description: Seoul apartment prices have continued to rise, although the pace of increase has slowed to its weakest in approximately five months. Bloomberg posted on X that this deceleration is attributed to the Lunar New Year holiday, which has dampened demand. The upcoming central bank meeting next week, where interest rates will be reviewed, is also influencing the market. The real estate sector is closely monitoring these developments as they could impact future price trends.

data.title: Fast-Fashion Market Faces New Competition from Shein and Temu
data.description:
Shein and Temu are making significant strides in the fast-fashion industry, challenging Primark's longstanding dominance. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing influence of these online retailers in the market. Shein, known for its trendy and affordable clothing, has rapidly expanded its customer base, leveraging social media and influencer partnerships to boost its visibility. Temu, a newer entrant, is also gaining traction by offering competitive prices and a wide range of products.

Primark, traditionally a leader in the fast-fashion sector, is facing increased pressure as these digital platforms attract a younger, tech-savvy audience. The shift towards online shopping has been accelerated by the pandemic, with consumers increasingly favoring convenience and variety offered by e-commerce.

Industry experts note that the competition is intensifying, with Shein and Temu investing heavily in marketing and logistics to enhance their global reach. This trend is reshaping the fast-fashion landscape, prompting established brands like Primark to adapt their strategies to maintain market share.

As the battle for dominance continues, the fast-fashion industry is witnessing a transformation driven by digital innovation and changing consumer preferences. The emergence of Shein and Temu signifies a new era in retail, where online platforms are becoming formidable contenders against traditional brick-and-mortar stores.

data.title: Jollibee Foods to Acquire Korean Hot Pot Brand for $87 Million
data.description: Jollibee Foods, the largest restaurant operator in the Philippines, announced its decision to acquire a Korean hot pot brand for approximately $87 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move as part of Jollibee's expansion efforts in the international market. The acquisition is expected to enhance Jollibee's portfolio and strengthen its presence in the Asian food industry. This deal marks a significant step for the company as it continues to diversify its offerings and expand its global footprint.

data.title: Amazon Faces Service Disruptions Due to AI Tool Failures
data.description: Amazon's cloud division has experienced at least two service interruptions due to failures in its artificial intelligence tools, raising concerns among some employees about the company's aggressive promotion of these programming assistants. According to Jin10, the disruptions have led to questions regarding the reliability of the AI tools and their impact on Amazon's cloud services. The incidents highlight the challenges faced by tech companies in integrating advanced AI technologies into their operations.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Warns Against AI Feedback Gap Risks
data.description: Vitalik Buterin has expressed concerns about the widening human-AI feedback gap promoted by Sigil’s Web4.0, cautioning that it could lead to low-quality outputs and potential irreversible risks. According to NS3.AI, Buterin reaffirmed Ethereum's mission to empower humans rather than develop independent AI systems that may worsen human conditions. He pointed out that current AI models, such as those from OpenAI and Anthropic, reinforce centralized trust, which conflicts with Ethereum's decentralized ethos. Buterin emphasized the importance of guiding AI development wisely rather than hastening its growth.

data.title: Eric Trump Expresses Optimism About Bitcoin's Future
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's son, Eric Trump, recently shared his optimistic views on Bitcoin during an interview with CNBC. According to Ming Pao, Eric Trump stated that he has never been more confident about Bitcoin, predicting that its value will eventually reach $1 million.

data.title: Circle Developer Introduces Nanopayments Solution for Seamless Transactions
data.description: Circle Developer has unveiled a new permissionless payment solution, Nanopayments, in its beta version. According to Foresight News, this innovative system is powered by Circle Gateway and facilitates USDC transfers without gas fees, allowing transactions as small as $0.000001. The solution is designed for various applications, including autonomous payments by AI agents, usage-based billing, machine-to-machine value settlement, streaming payments, and programmable internet commerce. Developers can now apply for early access to this cutting-edge technology.

data.title: Custodia Bank CEO Highlights Challenges in U.S. CLARITY Act Passage
data.description: Custodia Bank CEO Caitlin Long has pointed to controversies involving U.S. President Donald Trump's family and related meme coins as factors complicating bipartisan support for the U.S. CLARITY Act. According to NS3.AI, the bill, which seeks to establish clear regulatory frameworks for digital assets, is experiencing delays in the Senate. These delays are attributed to disputes over decentralized finance (DeFi), stablecoins, and political opposition stemming from ethics concerns. Despite these challenges and ongoing market volatility, Long remains optimistic about the long-term prospects of crypto legislation and emphasizes the educational value of bear markets.

data.title: A newly created address withdrew 7,000 ETH from Binance in the past hour, worth $13.55 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created address withdrew 7,000 ETH from Binance in the past hour, worth $13.55 million. This address currently holds 7,100 ETH, worth approximately $13.74 million.

data.title: Standard Chartered Initiates Sale of Botswana Business
data.description: Standard Chartered has commenced the sale of its Botswana operations, with initial bids anticipated by mid-year, according to sources. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing the bank's strategic move to streamline its operations. The decision aligns with Standard Chartered's broader strategy to focus on markets with higher growth potential. The sale process is expected to attract interest from various bidders, reflecting the bank's efforts to optimize its global footprint. The move comes as part of Standard Chartered's ongoing evaluation of its business portfolio, aiming to enhance efficiency and profitability.

data.title: Polymarket Faces Hack Due to Order System Flaw
data.description:
Polymarket, a prediction market platform, has been targeted by hackers exploiting a design flaw in its order system synchronization mechanism. According to ChainCatcher, the attackers manipulated the nonce to cancel or invalidate on-chain matched trades before they were finalized, while off-chain records remained valid. This discrepancy led to API errors affecting trading bots like Negrisk, resulting in user losses.

The attack involved several steps: first, the attackers submitted or matched large reverse trades with market-making bots on Polymarket's off-chain order book. They then constructed transactions with forged or duplicate nonces or engaged in nonce competition on-chain, ensuring the on-chain transactions would revert. The Polymarket API prematurely reported a 'successful trade' to the bots before on-chain confirmation, causing the bots to believe their positions were hedged, although the actual on-chain status had not changed.

Subsequently, the attackers executed real on-chain trades to exploit the exposed positions of the bots, profiting without risk. Since the reversion occurred at the chain level, Polymarket's fees did not escalate, keeping attack costs manageable and allowing continuous execution.

GoPlus advises users to halt automated trading tools, verify on-chain transaction statuses, enhance wallet security, and closely monitor official announcements from Polymarket.
